title: They Won't Frown Always, -- Some Sweet Day"
content:
They won't frown always, -- some sweet day
    When I forget to tease,
    They'll recollect how cold I looked,
    And how I just said 'please.'

    Then they will hasten to the door
    To call the little child,
    Who cannot thank them, for the ice
    That on her lisping piled.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "They Won't Frown Always, -- Some Sweet Day"", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
